scope: Short parity-only batch for Infusion Matrix catalyst NBT/damage lock, Arcane Workbench virtual output hardening, and explicit progress tracking.

porting_progress_estimate.basis: Functional TC4 parity bucket estimate, not a file-count metric. Full runtime parity still needs GitHub/local Gradle build and in-game smoke testing.

plan_used: Fix high-risk runtime duplication/drift before adding any broader features.
Preserve v7.82 Infusion Matrix failure/stabilizer/symmetry and Thaumometer scan fixes.
Add a progress document and next-batch plan so future answers include percent-to-100 tracking.
changes: InfusionMatrixBlockEntity now stores lockedCatalystSnapshot as an exact ItemStack copy at craft start, matching TC4 TileInfusionMatrix recipeInput semantics.
Running catalyst validation now calls lockedCatalystStackStillMatches(...), checking item id, damage wildcard semantics, and NBT against the locked recipe input copy.
Infusion Matrix save/load writes and reads LockedCatalystSnapshot plus legacy-style recipeinput for audit and migration parity.
TC4InfusionRuntime now exposes sameCraftingCatalyst(...) for strict TC4 recipeInput comparison in modern 1.19.2 ItemStack terms.
ArcaneWorkbenchBlockEntity now treats SLOT_OUTPUT as virtual preview only: it is skipped during save, cleared on load, and excluded from block-break drops.
ArcaneWorkbenchBlock now calls dropRealContents(...) instead of generic Containers.dropContents(...), preventing stale preview duplication.
docs/PORTING_PROGRESS_V8_02.md records the working estimate: 72% complete / 28% remaining.
docs/NEXT_CHAT_PROMPT_V8_02.md defines next v8.22 targets and requires progress/plan reporting in future responses.
remaining_to_100_percent: Infusion Matrix exact component source locks, cancellation/failure semantics, source FX and special recipe edge cases.
Arcane Workbench vanilla remaining-items parity, exact slot interaction/hover/sound behaviour.
Essentia/alchemy/golem/eldritch/worldgen runtime QA and final jar/in-game smoke testing.
compile_note: Gradle wrapper compile may fail in restricted sandboxes if external DNS/network access to services.gradle.org is blocked. Run GitHub Actions/local Gradle to build the jar. Static Java syntax guard passes.
